MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party Emilo Valdivia

RULING:
DEFENDANT DAP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant DAP, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-5 (exposure/causation) and off calendar as to issues 6-8 (false representation, civil battery and punitive damages). Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration and defendant's responses to General Order interrogatories create a triable issue whether plaintiff worked with and sanded DAP asbestos-containing Architectural Caulk. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production on the issue of whether plaintiff's sanding of the asbestos-containing DAP products released respirable fibers. Plaintiff's objections to Mr. Treat's declaration and Mr. Spencer's declaration are sustained. Assuming plaintiff was exposed to DAP's asbestos-containing products, Dr. Bruch's declaration creates a triable issue whether such exposure was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his disease. Defendant's objections to Dr. Bruch's declaration are overruled. Plaintiff represented in his response separate statement that he has dismissed the false representation and civil battery causes of action and his claim for punitive damages, although the court sees no dismissal on file. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jennifer L. Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff
/ opposing party Emilo Valdivia

RULING:
DEFENDANT HIGHLAND STUCCO AND LIME PRODUCTS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Highland Stucco and Lime Products, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration, defendant's responses to General Order 129 interrogatories and Mr. Atkinson's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Highland color coat stucco and Oyster White acoustical plaster) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's declaration and its own General Order interrogatory responses are overruled. The court only considered this as a motion for summary judgment. Although defendant's caption titles it a motion for summary judgment or in the alternative motion for summary adjudication, defendant's notice and separate statement failed to state the specific causes of action or issues sought to be adjudicated in violation of CRC 3.1350(b), (d). Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
